name: Vertical Microdrama
description: Template for vertical microdrama episodes (60-90 seconds, ReelShort style) — hook in 3 seconds, one plot, one reversal, mandatory cliffhanger. Built for 50-100 episode seasons watched on a phone.

subsections.name: Idea Wizard
subsections.description: Develop this microdrama episode. A vertical episode is not a short film: it is one beat of a bingeable chain — grab in the first 3 seconds, deliver one turn, and end on a hook that forces the next tap. If the project belongs to a Show, the series bible and the season map feed the discussion.

fields.label: Genre / Trope
fields.placeholder: e.g., Secret billionaire, Revenge, Contract marriage, Hidden identity...

fields.label: Episode Idea
fields.placeholder: What single thing happens in THIS 60-90s episode? One situation, one turn.

subsections.chat_system_prompt: You are an expert vertical-microdrama development partner (ReelShort / DramaBox style). Help the user develop ONE episode of 60-90 seconds. The rules are strict: hook in the first 3 seconds (a slap, a reveal, a threat, a humiliation), ONE plotline only (no B-plot), exactly ONE reversal per episode, and a MANDATORY cliffhanger that makes the viewer tap 'next'. Emotions are big and legible; every line either escalates the situation or twists it. Push the user to cut anything that isn't the hook, the turn, or the cliffhanger. Think in taps, not minutes.

subsections.name: Import Episode
subsections.description: Bring an existing microdrama script to seed beats, scenes, and the screenplay editor. Paste or upload it, optionally include guidance, and we will align beats and scenes for you.

fields.label: Guidance (Optional)
fields.placeholder: Any specific guidance for how to align beats and scenes...

subsections.name: Core
subsections.description: Lock the engine of this microdrama episode. One hook, one turn, one cliffhanger. If you can't say the hook and the cliffhanger in one line each, the episode isn't ready.

cards.label: Episode Logline
cards.placeholder: This episode in one breath: who, the single situation, and the turn.

cards.label: 3-Second Hook
cards.placeholder: What lands in the first 3 seconds to stop the scroll — a slap, a reveal, a threat, a humiliation.

cards.label: The One Plotline
cards.placeholder: The single thread this episode runs. No B-plot. Who wants what, right now.

cards.label: The Reversal
cards.placeholder: The one turn that flips the situation this episode — the reason the viewer feels something changed.

cards.label: Cliffhanger (mandatory)
cards.placeholder: The unresolved threat, reveal or question that forces the next tap. Never optional.

cards.label: Emotional Payload
cards.placeholder: The big, legible feeling this episode delivers — satisfaction, outrage, vindication, dread.

cards.label: Series Questions Advanced
cards.placeholder: Which ongoing questions inch forward here without closing (the reason to keep bingeing).

cards.label: Theme (optional)
cards.placeholder: The idea underneath the melodrama, if any. Optional — the turn matters more.

cards.label: Series-Arc Shift
cards.placeholder: What is different in the story when this episode ends. If nothing changed, it's filler — cut it.

subsections.name: Characters
subsections.description: Who carries this episode. Vertical drama runs on a tiny set of high-contrast characters: the driver, the one who opposes them this episode, and whoever the turn lands on. Keep it small — a phone screen holds one face at a time.

fields.label: Core Trait
fields.placeholder: The single legible quality driving them this episode.

fields.label: What They Want (this episode)
fields.placeholder: The immediate, concrete thing they push for right now.

fields.label: Dialogue Style
fields.placeholder: How they speak — short, punchy, quotable.

fields.label: What They Want
fields.placeholder: Their agenda this episode — the thing that collides with the driver.

fields.label: Role This Episode
fields.placeholder: What they do to feed the turn or the cliffhanger.

subsections.name: Beats
subsections.description: Structure the 60-90 seconds: hook in the first 3 seconds, escalate the single situation fast, land the one reversal, and cut on the cliffhanger. There is no denouement — the episode ends the instant the hook for the next one is set.

cards.label: Hook (first 3 seconds)
cards.placeholder: Open ON the moment of maximum charge. No runway — the stop-the-scroll beat is the first frame.

cards.label: Fast Setup
cards.placeholder: In a few lines: who's here, what's at stake right now. Compress ruthlessly.

cards.label: Escalation
cards.placeholder: The single situation tightens — pressure rises toward the turn. One line at a time, no detours.

cards.label: The Reversal
cards.placeholder: The turn lands: a reveal, a betrayal, a power flip. The emotional payload hits here.

cards.label: Cliffhanger (Out)
cards.placeholder: Cut on the new threat/question the reversal opens. The last frame IS the reason to tap next. Mandatory.

subsections.name: Scene Wizard
subsections.description: A microdrama episode is usually 1-3 short scenes. The wizard turns the beats into a tight scene list that hits the hook fast and ends on the cliffhanger.

wizard_config.guidance_default: Construct scenes for a 60-90 second vertical microdrama episode. Open ON the hook in the first scene's first line — no establishing runway. Keep to ONE plotline; every scene either escalates it or delivers the reversal. The final scene must cut on the cliffhanger. Favor short, quotable dialogue and a single face on screen at a time. No resolution, no wind-down.
wizard_config.beat_mapping_strategy: Map the beats into 1-3 scenes: the hook and fast setup open scene one; escalation may share that scene or spill to a second; the reversal lands late; the cliffhanger is the final beat of the final scene. Never end a microdrama episode on a resolved beat.

subsections.name: Individual Scenes
subsections.description: Craft each scene for the phone: enter on the charged moment, keep one face in frame, and leave on the beat that pulls forward. In a microdrama the 'turning point' is the reversal and the final scene's 'push forward' is the cliffhanger.

fields.label: Summary
fields.placeholder: What happens — kept to the single plotline.

fields.label: Arena
fields.placeholder: One location, one face at a time.

fields.label: Scene Hook
fields.placeholder: The charged moment the scene opens on — enter as late and as hot as possible.

fields.label: Goal
fields.placeholder: What the driver pushes for right now.

fields.label: Subtext
fields.placeholder: The feeling under the line — big but unspoken.

fields.label: Peak Beat
fields.placeholder: The hardest hit of the scene.

fields.label: Out / Cliffhanger
fields.placeholder: The pull into the next scene — and on the final scene, the episode cliffhanger.
